query:
I'm debating here on this.  Here is a list of the knowledge written by the retro architect.  Knowledge extracted:

  ┌───────┬─────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   #   │      Type       │                                    Title                                    │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3752 │ pattern (new)   │ Node-ID-sorted accumulation for deterministic graph scoring                 │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3753 │ pattern (new)   │ Use pre-cloned snapshot for new pipeline steps — never re-acquire lock      │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3756 │ pattern (new)   │ Wave structure: parallel independents first, sequential dependents second   │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3759 │ procedure (new) │ How to add InferenceConfig fields (incl. TOML Deserialize-only gotcha)      │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3754 │ lesson (new)    │ Graph algorithm direction semantics: conceptual direction ≠ Direction enum  │
  │       │                 │ value                                                                       │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3755 │ lesson (new)    │ Large file compile-cycle amplification — batch type defs before first       │
  │       │                 │ compile                                                                     │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3760 │ lesson (new)    │ context_store failures cluster during heavy cargo compilation (contention   │
  │       │                 │ hypothesis)                                                                 │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3757 │ lesson          │ Agent stewardship section — 4th instance; structural grep check recommended │
  │       │ (updated)       │                                                                             │
  ├───────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ #3758 │ lesson          │ Agents defaulting to Bash grep — 4th above-threshold feature; not           │
  │       │ (updated)       │ self-correcting                                                             │
  └───────┴─────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\
Take for example 3757.  Its a valuable lesson the human should react to to improve the process (one of the core reasons of the retro itself).  Its valuable.. at a point in time.  Compared against decisions, patterns, etc... its value will diminish over time.  I'm trying to decide STRATEGICALLY, in a generic knowledge platform that supports many domains... how we want to address this issue.
